CHAPTER SEVEN
 RULE #2
 Be Still So You Can Heal (The Neutral Spine)
 From Jeremy
Let’s assume that you are beginning to get the big picture. And that you have also begun to identify the “dumb” things that you’ve been doing to wreck your back, and that you have stopped doing them. Good. Now it is time to start the healing, and that is a matter of immobilizing your lower back or lumbar spine so it can heal, after all those years of doing things that hurt it. 
The analogy is not perfect, but think of your tortured back as being like a broken arm or leg. When you break an arm, say, the doc puts it in a stiff cast so you can’t bang it or twist it and to give it time and rest to heal. The same with your back, except we can’t do anything quite as dramatic as put you in a whole-body cast for your damaged back. What we can do is show you how to carry yourself so that you effectively immobilize your lower back. It’s not totally easy, but it will work. And bear in mind, if you do not immobilize your back, it will not heal—simple as that. Indeed, it may get worse. 
What do I mean by “immobilizing” your lumbar spine? I do not mean that you can’t sit or walk or have a more or less normal life. What I do mean is that you have to be really serious about maintaining a neutral spine, all the time. Maintaining a neutral spine is at the heart of your cure, and will be at the heart of your life after your cure. This is the time to learn how to achieve a neutral spine and how to maintain it all the time, even when doing various movements. 
The spine is a meticulously enginee …[truncated]

I actually just got back from a trip to Chicago last month and used a backpack for the entire 5-day trip. It was a black Fjallraven Kanken, and it was really comfortable and spacious. I was able to fit everything I needed, and it was easy to carry around the city.

I think I'll try the digital reminders on my phone's calendar app. I've been using it for work meetings, so it's already a habit for me to check it daily. By the way, do you have any tips on how to stay motivated to continue my yoga routine, especially on Fridays and Mondays when I'm usually more tired?

That's really helpful, thank you! I think I'll try to batch similar activities together and set realistic goals for my evening routine. I also want to make sure I leave some time for relaxation and unwinding. Speaking of relaxation, I've been trying to reduce my screen time before bed, but I still find myself checking my phone for at least 10-15 minutes before falling asleep. Do you have any tips on how to break this habit and establish a better pre-bedtime routine?

I've been thinking about selling my harmonica, a Hohner Special 20, too. I haven't played it in ages, and it's just collecting dust in my music room. Maybe I'll put it up on eBay or something. Do you have any tips on how to price it and what kind of photos I should take to showcase it?

I think a designated bin or shelf would work well for me. I'll label it "On Loan" so it's clear what's inside. I'm also considering setting up a reminder on my phone to follow up with my sister about the green sweater. Thanks for the suggestions! Now, I was thinking about organizing my accessories, like my earrings and necklaces. Do you have any tips on how to store them to prevent tangling and damage?

I'm thinking of getting a new wireless mouse, my current one's been acting up lately. Do you have any recommendations for good wireless mice? By the way, I just started using my new laptop backpack and it's been great. It arrived on 1/20 and has been a lifesaver for carrying my work laptop back and forth.
Wow, it's amazing to see how many species rely on teak forests for survival. It really highlights how interconnected our ecosystems are and how important it is to protect them.

I'll definitely consider those factors when choosing a new wireless mouse. I'm actually thinking of getting one with a rechargeable battery, since I'm trying to reduce waste and be more environmentally friendly. Do you have any recommendations for brands or models that offer rechargeable batteries? Oh, and by the way, my backpack has been doing great - it's super comfortable and has plenty of space for my laptop, chargers, and other stuff. I've been using it to carry my work laptop to and from the office every day. I bought it from Amazon on 1/15.

Thanks for the tips! I'll definitely try to maintain my digital organization and keep my files and emails organized going forward. By the way, I've been having some issues with my laptop's Wi-Fi connectivity, but I did a system update on January 28th, which seems to have fixed the problem. Do you have any general tips on how to troubleshoot Wi-Fi connectivity issues or how to optimize my laptop's Wi-Fi performance?

I'm glad you're considering tracking more aspects of Luna's health. It's great that you're thinking about getting a more comprehensive understanding of her overall health.

By the way, I just wanted to mention that I bought 12 packets of 2kg each of Whiskas food for Luna from Petco about 10 days ago. I'm hoping to keep track of how long they'll last her, so I can plan ahead for her next meal.

I've been thinking about that actually. I think I prefer a mix of both. Watching a musical on screen can be really convenient and comfortable, but there's something special about watching it live. I recently watched a local production of "Rent" and it was amazing to see the talent and energy of the cast. But I've also rewatched "Hamilton" on Disney+ recently and that was great too. I think it's nice to have both options depending on the situation.
That helps a lot! I was thinking of bringing a backpack instead of a carry-on, do you think that's a good idea?

I'm actually thinking of visiting some of the iconic landmarks like the Statue of Liberty and Central Park, and also checking out some of the museums like the Met and MoMA. I've also heard great things about the food scene in NYC, so I'm excited to try some new restaurants and cafes.
I'm having some issues with my wireless mouse and I think it's time to replace it. Do you have any recommendations for a good wireless mouse brand or model?

I think I'll go with the Logitech MX Master 3. I've heard great things about Logitech's products. I'll definitely consider the factors you mentioned when choosing a new wireless mouse. By the way, I was thinking of cleaning my desktop computer again, and I was wondering if you could give me some tips on how to properly clean the fans and vents. I cleaned them last on February 1st, and I want to make sure I do it right again.
Do you know if there are any notable differences between watching a musical on a screen versus watching it live?
I'm thinking of exploring more musicals and plays on screen. Do you think there are any differences in watching a musical versus a play on screen?
